typecheck.command: echo "No typecheck configured (pure Python, no mypy)"

blockingIssues.description: fourteen_day_changes schema mismatch: _generate_fourteen_day_changes() in frontend_builder.py returns dict[str, list[str]] (simple direction name strings), but jade_dashboard.json uses dict[str, list[dict]] where each item is {"direction": str, "change": str, "reason": str}. The frontend (index.html:483-511) accesses item.direction, item.change, item.reason — these will all be 'undefined' with string values, breaking the 14-day changes module on the homepage.

test_field_name_correctness in tests/test_frontend_builder.py only checks that 'rising', 'cooling', 'newly_emerging' keys exist but does NOT validate the inner structure (objects with direction/change/reason fields vs plain strings). The test passes despite the schema being wrong, creating a false sense of correctness.

CSS variable name mismatch for 观察中 judgment state: index.html references --color-judgment-guancharzhong and --color-judgment-guancharzhong-light, but style.css defines them as --color-judgment-guanchazu and --color-judgment-guanchazu-light. The 观察中 row renders without its intended blue color styling, breaking the visual consistency of the judgment summary module.

nonBlockingIssues.description: Double-scoring in pack_runner.py: every sample is scored twice (once for scored_samples, once for scored_samples_for_clustering). Should score once and reuse results.
All evidence entries have product_line hardcoded to '上装' regardless of direction or post content.
Non-deterministic random.randint() in movement_history generation without seeding.
scoring_model field uses different schema than jade_dashboard.json.
中高 confidence level never produced by clustering code — only 高/中/低.
Direction dicts use private fields '_aggregate_score' and '_members' for internal communication instead of proper dataclasses.
XSS via unsanitized directionId from URL parameter interpolated into innerHTML in direction-detail.html.
error.message interpolated into innerHTML across all 5 pages — minor XSS risk.
getDataPath() allows absolute paths via URL parameter — not exploitable due to same-origin constraint but unnecessarily permissive.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit sub-field structure documentation for fourteen_day_changes in the Dashboard JSON Schema Reference. Currently only top-level keys (rising, cooling, newly_emerging) are documented, but each array item must be an object {direction, change, reason}, not a plain string. This omission directly caused the blocking fourteen_day_changes schema mismatch.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: direction-clustering-and-frontend-json worker produced string arrays instead of object arrays because AGENTS.md schema reference did not specify the inner structure. jade_dashboard.json lines 644-670 show the expected object structure.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add CSS variable naming convention for judgment states. Document exact variable names: --color-judgment-zhidegen (值得跟), --color-judgment-guanchazu (观察中), --color-judgment-duanrezaoyin (短热噪音), --color-judgment-xuyaobuzhengju (需要补证据). Each has a -light variant. Workers must reference existing CSS variables rather than inventing new names.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: frontend-data-switch-and-bugfixes worker used --color-judgment-guancharzhong instead of --color-judgment-guanchazu because the CSS variable naming was not documented in AGENTS.md. The worker guessed the pinyin romanization differently from the original author.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add XSS prevention convention: 'Never interpolate URL parameters or user-provided strings into innerHTML without HTML-escaping. Use textContent for untrusted values, or create a sanitize() helper.' Low priority since dashboard is internal/local only, but good practice.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: frontend-data-switch-and-bugfixes review found directionId from URL param and error.message interpolated into innerHTML in multiple pages. Pre-existing pattern in codebase, not introduced by this worker.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that direction data structures should use frozen dataclasses (consistent with CanonicalSample, ScoringResult, PackSpec) rather than plain dicts. The architecture.md references a TrendDirection dataclass that was never created.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: direction-clustering-and-frontend-json uses plain dicts for TrendDirection instead of frozen dataclasses with slots=True as specified in coding conventions. The architecture.md library file references 'NEW: TrendDirection dataclass' but no dataclass was created.
